content.clpd_hero_desc: The 11th year of Dubai's premier legal development programme. 4,411 licensed practitioners. 69 accredited courses. Build your expertise, meet your compliance requirements, and advance your career in law.
content.clpd_cta_title: Ready to start your CLPD journey?
content.clpd_cta_desc: Every licensed legal consultant in Dubai must register on the CLPD roll and complete 16 points by 31 December 2026.
content.lawyer_lex_card1_body: You have not yet booked an Ethics course this cycle. The free AI Governance e-learning fulfils Ethics, takes 4 hours, and is available immediately. Booking today closes one of your highest-risk gaps.
content.lawyer_lex_card2_body: Data Protection (25 Mar) and International Arbitration (1 Apr) cleared. Advanced Mediation (17 Mar) cancelled and AI & Copyright (13 Apr) is still pending. The pending session is worth 2 pts once Hadef files attendance.
content.lawyer_lex_card3_title: 12 points to clear in 234 days · realistic
content.lawyer_lex_card3_body: Three free e-learning modules plus one DIFC face-to-face (28 May) would deliver 12 points by end of June. Lex recommends booking the DIFC Day immediately — only 3 of 60 seats remain.
content.intel_desc: Real-time compliance across 499 firms and 4,411 lawyers in the 2026 cycle.
content.contact_address:
Government of Dubai · Legal Affairs Department
Al Hudaiba Awards Building, Floor 7
Dubai, UAE
content.lawyer_welcome_desc: Lex, your AI compliance coach, can personalise your CLPD journey — recommending courses, tracking your skill growth, and predicting the fastest path to 16 points.
content.accred_intro: Become an accredited CLPD training provider for the Government of Dubai. Submit Form A1 to apply for provider status and Form A2 for course-by-course accreditation.
content.accred_a1_title: Apply to become an accredited CLPD provider
content.accred_a1_desc: Submit your organisation profile, training history, and certifications. LAD reviews applications within 30 working days. Annual provider fee: AED 5,000.
content.accred_a2_desc: Submit each course you wish to register on the CLPD catalogue. Includes syllabus, learning outcomes, trainer CV, and proposed assessment. LAD reviews within 21 working days. Per-course fee: AED 1,200.

faq.q: How many CLPD points do I need?
faq.a: You need 16 points per calendar year — at least 8 from mandatory (LAD-defined) courses and 8 from accredited activities. Ethics is mandatory (2 pts minimum).
The current cycle ends 31 December 2026 at 23:59 GST. Filings submitted after the deadline incur an extension fee and may result in your DBA being marked non-compliant.

faq.q: How do I pay for courses?
faq.a: Courses are paid in CLPD credits. 1 credit = AED 120. All courses cost 5 credits. Buy credits from your dashboard with Visa or Mastercard. Firms can purchase a credit pool and assign credits to lawyers.

faq.q: Can I get an extension or exemption?
faq.a: Yes — see the Exemption / Extension page. Valid grounds include illness, maternity, military service, and continuous practice outside the UAE.
For face-to-face courses, the provider files an attendance record with LAD within 5 days. E-learning auto-records on completion.

faq.q: I am a new lawyer — when does my CLPD start?
faq.a: You enter the CLPD programme on 1 January following the year of your admission to the bar.

faq.q: What if my firm has a credit pool?
faq.a: Your firm compliance officer can assign credits to you directly. You can also purchase your own credits.

faq.q: I need to file a pro bono record
faq.a: Pro bono work counts towards your CLPD if registered through the Dubai Pro Bono Programme.
